title: Unique Algerian Pinto Bean Tagine
description: A spicy and molten Algerian dish featuring pinto beans and confit tomato, served on a hot stone plate, perfect for a brunch mood, this recipe is also anti-inflammatory friendly. It combines the richness of pinto beans with the sweetness of confit tomatoes.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pan and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Stir in the ground cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, black pepper, and turmeric, cooking for 1 minute.
Add the soaked and drained pinto beans to the pan, stirring to coat with the spice mixture.
Add the confit tomatoes to the pan, stirring gently to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a hot stone plate or a ceramic tagine with a lid.
Cover the dish with a lid or foil and bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid or foil and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the beans are tender and the top is lightly browned.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
